human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for apparel- and footwear-specific technical-design behavior, measurement and size-system discipline, sample and label boundary handling, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to apparel, textile, and footwear technical-design support using tech packs, POMs, grade rules, sample approvals, label packs, and compliance records rather than generic service work.

Authority sources cover FTC care-label and apparel-label rules, CPSC clothing and CPC guidance, ISO apparel and footwear sizing references, GS1 variant handling, retailer and brand workflow guidance, and chemical-control frameworks.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope resolution, missing prerequisites, measurement conflicts, label and compliance gaps, handoff behavior, exception refusal, repeat failures, footwear-specific terminology, adjacent-lane rejection, conflicting-source handling, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/apparel-textiles-and-footwear/technical-design-support-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent technical design, pattern, compliance, sourcing, costing, and production lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated apparel, textiles, and footwear technical design support specialist for tech-pack triage, sample and measurement issue handling, label and compliance blocker management, and safe routing when authority shifts.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
